#  {background-image="images/DeborahLupton-Servers-Landscape-2560x3620.png" background-size="cover" style="font-size: 70%;" align="center"}

::: {.newsection style="--h1-banner-color: #6C8864AA; --h1-banner-text-color: #DFC65E"}
Designing data science & AI platforms
:::

## Our dream: a data system that just works
{{< source "the Composable Codex" url="https://web.archive.org/web/20251208133433/https://voltrondata.com/codex" >}}

<br>

![](images/dream-data-system.png)

## The problem {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "MAD landscape" url="https://mad.firstmark.com/" >}}

![](images/open-standards-mad-landscape.jpg)


## The Composable Data Management System Manifesto
{{< source "Pedreira et al. (2023)" url="https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.14778/3603581.3603604" >}}

<br>

::: {style="font-size: 80%"}
> _The requirement for specialization in data management systems has evolved faster than our software development practices. After decades of organic growth, this situation has created a **siloed landscape** composed of hundreds of products developed and maintained as monoliths, with limited reuse between systems. This fragmentation has resulted in developers often reinventing the wheel, **increased maintenance costs, and slowed down innovation**. It has also affected the end users, who are often required to learn the **idiosyncrasies of dozens of incompatible SQL and non-SQL API dialects**, and settle for systems with incomplete functionality and inconsistent semantics._
:::

## It all started with the relational database management system (RDBMS) {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "E.F. Codd (1970)" url="https://doi.org/10.1145/362384.362685" >}}

![](images/coddpaper.webp)


## What makes a database?

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![](images/db-catalog-schema.png)
:::
::: {.column style="font-size: 80%;"}

<br>

| System     | Catalog  | Database   |
|------------|----------|------|
| postgresql, mysql, mssql, duckdb | database | schema     |
| datafusion, trino | catalog        | schema     |
| druid      | dataSourceType | dataSource |
| bigquery   | project        | database   |
| flink      | catalog        | database   |
| clickhouse |                | database   |
| clickhouse, impala, mysql, pyspark, snowflake |      | database   |
:::
:::

## Comparing operational and analytical data systems
{{< source "Kleppmann & Riccomini (2026), chapter 1" url="https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/designing-data-intensive-applications/9781098119058/" >}}

::: {style="font-size: 70%;"}
|Property|Operational systems (OLTP)|Analytic systems (OLAP)|
|---|-------|------|
|Main read pattern|Point queries (fetch individual records by key)|Aggregate over large number of records|
|Main write pattern|Create, update, and delete individual records|Bulk import (ETL) or event stream|
|Human user example|End user of web/mobile application|Internal analyst, for decision support|
|Type of queries|Fixed, predefined by application|Arbitrary, ad-hoc exploration by analysts|
|Query volume|Lost of small queries|Few queries, each is complex|
|Data represents|Latest state of data (current point in time)|History of events that happened over time|
|Dataset size|Gigabytes to terabytes|Terabytes to petabytes|
:::

## Evolution of analytical system architectures
{{< source "Armbrust et al. (2021)" url="https://15721.courses.cs.cmu.edu/spring2023/papers/02-modern/armbrust-cidr21.pdf" >}}

![](images/generations-data-plattforms.png)

## Opening up the data warehouses
{{< source "Kleppmann & Riccomini (2026), chapter 4" url="https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/designing-data-intensive-applications/9781098119058/" >}}

:::{style="font-size: 60%"}
::: {.list-table tbl-colwidths="[25,40,35]"}
- - Component
  - Function
  - Open source software

- - Open **query** engine
  - Parse SQL queries, optimize them into execution plans, and execute them against the data
  - [Apache DataFusion](https://datafusion.apache.org/)<br>[Apache Spark](https://spark.apache.org/)<br>[DuckDB](https://duckdb.org/)

- - Open **catalog** format
  - Defines which tables are contained in the database
  - [Apache Iceberg](https://iceberg.apache.org/)<br>[Unity catalog](https://www.unitycatalog.io/)<br>[Lance](https://lance.org/)

- - Open **table** format
  - Support row inserts and deletions
  - [Apache Iceberg](https://iceberg.apache.org/)<br>[Lance](https://lance.org/)<br>[Delta Lake](https://delta.io/)<br>[Apache Hudi](https://hudi.apache.org/)

- - Open **storage** formats
  - Determines how the rows of a table are encoded as bytes in a file
  - [Apache Parquet](https://parquet.apache.org/)<br>[Lance](https://lance.org/)<br>[Apache Orc](https://orc.apache.org/docs/)

- - Open **memory** formats
  - Determines how the rows of a table are encoded as bytes in **memory**
  - [Apache Arrow](https://arrow.apache.org/)

:::
:::

::: {.notes}
Unbundling driven by move to the cloud

- cheap blob storage
- microservice architecture ("serverless")
:::

## From row-based to column-based systems
{{< source "Apache Arrow documentation" url="https://arrow.apache.org/overview/" >}}

![](images/row-vs-columnar-data.png)

## The Composable Data Stack takes the unbundling even further
{{< source "the Composable Codex" url="https://web.archive.org/web/20251208133433/https://voltrondata.com/codex" >}}

![](images/composable-data-stack.png)

## The Composable Data Stack takes the unbundling even further
{{< source "the Composable Codex" url="https://web.archive.org/web/20251208133433/https://voltrondata.com/codex" >}}

![](images/composable-data-stack-implementation-3.png)

## End-to-end columnar data with Apache Arrow ADBC and Flight SQL {background-color="#ffffff"}

![](images/parquet-arrow-iceberg.png)

## JDBC/ODBC: row-based database connectivity protocols {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "Apache Arrow project" url="https://arrow.apache.org/blog/2023/01/05/introducing-arrow-adbc/" >}}

![](images/adbc-flow-1.png)

## Arrow Database Connectivity (ADBC) is column-based {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "Apache Arrow project" url="https://arrow.apache.org/blog/2023/01/05/introducing-arrow-adbc/" >}}

![](images/adbc-flow-2.png)

## Iceberg: an open table format and catalog {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "Apache Iceberg specification" url="https://iceberg.apache.org/spec/" >}}

<br>

![](images/iceberg-metadata.png)

## Iceberg catalog {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "Dremio" url="https://www.dremio.com/resources/guides/apache-iceberg-an-architectural-look-under-the-covers" >}}

![](images/iceberg-catalog.webp)

## Iceberg metadata file {background-color="#ffffff"}
{{< source "Dremio" url="https://www.dremio.com/resources/guides/apache-iceberg-an-architectural-look-under-the-covers" >}}

![](images/iceberg-metadata-file.webp)

## Big Data Is Dead
{{< source "Jordan Tigani" url="https://motherduck.com/blog/big-data-is-dead/" >}}

![](images/big-data-is-dead.png){}

## The rise of embedded databases
{{< source "the Data Quarry blog" url="https://thedataquarry.com/posts/embedded-db-1/" >}}

:::{style="font-size: 60%"}
::: {.list-table}
- - Type of data
  - Open source embedable database

- - Multi-modal
  - - [LanceDB](https://www.lancedb.com/)
    - [CozoDB](https://www.cozodb.org/)

- - Relational
  - - [DuckDB](https://duckdb.org)

- - Key-value documents
  - - [RocksDB](https://rocksdb.org/)

- - Vector
  - - [Chroma](https://docs.trychroma.com/docs/overview/introduction)

- - Labeled-property graph with Cypher query engine
  - - [LadybudDB](https://ladybugdb.com/)

- - Tripe-store with SPARQL query engine
  - - [qlever](https://docs.qlever.dev/)

:::  
:::

## So has our dream come true?
{{< source "the Composable Codex" url="https://web.archive.org/web/20251208133433/https://voltrondata.com/codex" >}}

![](images/dream-data-system.png)

## We are getting very close ... 
{{< source "DuckLake" url="https://ducklake.select/" >}}

<br>

![](images/ducklake.png){fig-align="center"}

## Diagonal scaling of a DuckLake lakehouse
{{< source "Cloudzero" url="https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/horizontal-vs-vertical-scaling/" >}}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
![](images/scaling-type.png)
:::
::: {.column style="font-size: 80%;"}

<br>

- **Vertical scaling** of single node query engines that can process up to 100 TB, covering 99% of use cases
- **Horizontal scaling** of blob storage in (sovereign!) data centres

:::
:::

## From components to a whole platform architecture
{{< source "Andreesen Horowitz" url="https://a16z.com/emerging-architectures-for-modern-data-infrastructure/" >}}

![](images/Unified-Data-Infrastructure-2.0.png)

## From components to a whole platform architecture
{{< source "Andreesen Horowitz" url="https://a16z.com/emerging-architectures-for-modern-data-infrastructure/" >}}

![](images/Machine-Learning-Infrastructure-2.0.png)

## Introducing the Single-Repo Data Platform (SRDP)
{{< source "SRDP Hub" url="https://github.com/srdp-hub/srdp" >}}

![](images/srdp-architecture.png)
